html:
[807]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 饿鬼 - Literally means 'hungry ghost,' a term often used to describe someone who is extremely poor or desperate.
  • 日益窭 - Means 'gradually becoming poorer,' where '窭' (jù) refers to poverty.
  • 衣百结鹑 - Describes clothes that are patched together, indicating extreme poverty. '鹑' (chún) refers to a type of bird whose feathers are used for patches.
  • 两手交其肩 - Literally means 'both hands crossed on the shoulders,' which suggests a posture of helplessness or resignation.
  • 攫食 - Means 'to snatch food,' often implying stealing or taking food without paying.
  • 人尽弃之，不以齿 - Means 'people abandon him and do not consider him equal,' indicating social ostracism.
  • 操业不雅 - Means 'engaged in unrefined or disreputable work,' suggesting a low-status occupation.
  • 暮岁归其乡 - Means 'in old age he returned to his hometown,' indicating a return to one's roots.
  • 大为士类所口 - Means 'greatly criticized by scholars,' indicating negative social judgment.
  • 洁行为善 - Means 'clean and virtuous behavior,' indicating moral uprightness.
  • 人始稍稍礼貌之 - Means 'people began to show him some respect gradually,' indicating a change in social attitude.
  • 值马攫食不偿，为肆人所苦 - Means 'when Ma grabbed food without paying, he was troubled by the shopkeeper,' indicating a specific incident.
  • 怜之，代给其直 - Means 'pitying him, Zhu gave him the money,' indicating an act of kindness.
  • 引归，赠以数百俾作本 - Means 'brought him back, gave him several hundred coins to start a business,' indicating an attempt to help him improve his situation.
  • 马去，不肯谋业，坐而食 - Means 'Ma left, unwilling to plan a business, sitting and eating,' indicating a lack of initiative.
  • 无何资复匮 - Means 'soon his funds ran out again,' indicating a recurrence of poverty.
  • 仍蹈故辙 - Means 'still following the same old path,' indicating a failure to change his behavior.
  • 常惧与朱遇，去之临邑 - Means 'often fearing to meet Zhu, he went to the neighboring county,' indicating an avoidance of the person who helped him.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 齐人马永 - Refers to a person named Ma Yong from the state of Qi, indicating a specific historical context.
  • 乡人戏名为饿鬼 - Indicates a social practice of giving nicknames based on appearance or behavior.
  • 邑有朱叟者 - Refers to an elderly man named Zhu in the town, indicating a local figure.
  • 少携妻居于五都之市 - Indicates a specific location and lifestyle, suggesting a merchant or trader.
  • 操业不雅 - Refers to a type of work that is considered low-status or disreputable.
  • 暮岁归其乡 - Indicates a common life cycle pattern of returning to one's hometown in old age.
  • 大为士类所口 - Indicates the social judgment and criticism by scholars, reflecting the social hierarchy and values of the time.
  • 洁行为善 - Reflects the importance of moral uprightness in traditional Chinese society.
  • 人始稍稍礼貌之 - Indicates a gradual change in social attitudes, reflecting the impact of moral behavior.

Proper Nouns

  • 齐人马永 - A person named Ma Yong from the state of Qi.
  • 朱叟 - An elderly man named Zhu.
  • 五都之市 - A specific market or commercial area.
  • 临邑 - A neighboring county or town.

[808]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 暮宿學宮 - Literally 'dwell in the school at night,' referring to a student staying at the school overnight.
  • 冬夜凛寒 - Literally 'winter night is cold and severe,' describing the harsh winter weather.
  • 輒摘聖賢頭上旒而煨其板 - Literally 'often plucks the tassels from the heads of sages and roasts them on the boards.' This is a highly metaphorical and likely exaggerated description of the student's behavior.
  • 學官知之，怒欲加刑 - Literally 'the school official knew this and was angry, wanting to punish him.'
  • 馬哀免，願為先生生財 - Literally 'the horse mourned and begged for mercy, willing to make the teacher rich.' This is a metaphorical reference to the student's plea.
  • 學官喜，縱之去 - Literally 'the school official was pleased and let him go.'
  • 馬探其生殷富 - Literally 'the horse probed his life to be wealthy,' meaning the student investigated the teacher's wealth.
  • 登門強索資 - Literally 'went to the door to forcefully demand money.'
  • 故挑其怒 - Literally 'deliberately provoked his anger.'
  • 乃以刀自劙 - Literally 'then cut himself with a knife,' a dramatic act of self-harm.
  • 诬而控诸学 - Literally 'accused him falsely and reported him to the school.'
  • 學官勒取重赂 - Literally 'the school official extorted a large bribe.'
  • 始免申黜 - Literally 'only then was he spared from dismissal.'
  • 諸生因而共愤 - Literally 'the other students thus all became angry.'
  • 公质县尹 - Literally 'the magistrate investigated publicly.'
  • 尹廉得实 - Literally 'the magistrate investigated and found the truth.'
  • 笞四十，梏其颈 - Literally 'whipped forty times and shackled his neck.'
  • 三日毙焉 - Literally 'died after three days.'
  • 是夜，朱叟梦马冠带而入 - Literally 'that night, the old man Zhu dreamed of the horse wearing a hat and entering.'
  • 负公大德，今来相报 - Literally 'repaying a great favor, now comes to repay.'
  • 即寤，妾生子 - Literally 'awoke, and his wife gave birth to a son.'
  • 叟知为马，名以马儿 - Literally 'the old man knew it was the horse, and named the child 'Horse Son'.'
  • 少不慧 - Literally 'was not bright as a child.'
  • 喜其能读 - Literally 'was pleased that he could read.'
  • 二十余，竭力经纪，得入邑庠 - Literally 'in his twenties, worked hard and managed affairs, eventually entered the county school.'
  • 后考试寓旅邸，昼卧床上，见壁间悉糊旧艺 - Literally 'later, during an exam at a lodging, lay on the bed during the day and saw the walls covered with old essays.'
  • 视之有“犬之性”四句题，心畏其难，读而志之 - Literally 'saw the four-line question 'The Nature of Dogs,' was afraid of its difficulty, read it, and memorized it.'
  • 入场，适遇此题，录之，得优等，食饩焉 - Literally 'when entering the examination hall, just encountered this question, wrote it down, and received a top grade, thus receiving a stipend.'
  • 六十余，补临邑训导 - Literally 'in his sixties, was appointed as a teacher in Lin County.'
  • 数年，曾无一道义交 - Literally 'for several years, never had a single moral or ethical relationship.'
  • 惟袖中出青蚨，则作鸬鹚笑；不则睫毛一寸长，棱棱若不相识 - Literally 'only when he took out coins from his sleeve would he laugh like a cormorant; otherwise, his eyebrows would be a foot long, sharp and unrecognizable.'
  • 偶大令以诸生小故，判令薄惩，辄酷烈如治盗贼 - Literally 'once the magistrate punished a student for a minor offense, he would be extremely harsh, treating it like a criminal case.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 學宮 - Refers to a traditional Chinese school or academy.
  • 學官 - Refers to the school official or headmaster.
  • 聖賢 - Refers to sages and virtuous individuals, often used in Confucian contexts.
  • 旒 - The tassels or streamers on the headgear of sages, symbolizing their high status.
  • 邑庠 - Refers to the county school, a local educational institution.
  • 大令 - Refers to a high-ranking official, often the magistrate or county governor.
  • 青蚨 - Ancient Chinese coins, often used metaphorically for money.
  • 鸬鹚 - A type of waterbird, often used in literature to symbolize greed or cunning.

Proper Nouns

  • 朱叟 - The name of the old man Zhu.
  • 馬兒 - The name given to the son of Zhu, meaning 'Horse Son'.
  • 臨邑 - The name of the county where the character was appointed as a teacher.

[809]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 有讼士子者，即富来叩门矣 - Compound construction where '有讼士子者' means 'there is a scholar who sues,' and '即富来叩门矣' means 'and immediately the rich come knocking at the door.'
  • 如此多端 - Phrase indicating multiple ways or methods, often used to describe complex or varied situations.
  • 诸生不复可耐 - '诸生' refers to students or scholars, and '不复可耐' means 'can no longer bear it.'
  • 年近七旬，臃肿聋胔 - '年近七旬' means 'nearly seventy years old,' '臃肿' means 'fat and bloated,' and '聋胔' means 'deaf and dumb.'
  • 每向人物色乌须药 - '每向人物色' means 'often asks people for,' and '乌须药' means 'black hair medicine.'
  • 锉茜根给之 - '锉茜根' means 'grind the root of madder,' and '给之' means 'give it to him.'
  • 如庙中所塑灵官状 - '如庙中所塑' means 'like those carved in temples,' and '灵官状' means 'the appearance of a divine official.'
  • 大怒拘生 - '大怒' means 'very angry,' and '拘生' means 'detain the student.'
  • 生已早夜亡去 - '早夜亡去' means 'fled early in the night.'
  • 因此愤气中结，数月而死 - '愤气中结' means 'anger accumulated internally,' and '数月而死' means 'died after several months.'
  • 闻人生，河南人 - '闻人生' is a proper name, and '河南人' means 'from Henan.'
  • 抱病经日 - '抱病' means 'suffering from illness,' and '经日' means 'for several days.'
  • 伏谒床下，谦抑尽礼 - '伏谒' means 'prostrate oneself,' '床下' means 'under the bed,' '谦抑' means 'humble and modest,' and '尽礼' means 'with utmost respect.'
  • 把臂长语，刺刺且行 - '把臂' means 'hold arms,' '长语' means 'long conversation,' and '刺刺且行' means 'chattering as they walk.'
  • 数里外犹不言别 - '数里外' means 'several miles away,' and '犹不言别' means 'still not saying goodbye.'
  • 伫足，拱手致辞 - '伫足' means 'stand still,' '拱手' means 'bow hands,' and '致辞' means 'deliver a message.'
  • 更烦移趾，仆有一事相求 - '更烦移趾' means 'please move a little more,' and '仆有一事相求' means 'I have something to ask of you.'
  • 吾辈悉属考弊司辖 - '吾辈' means 'we,' '悉属' means 'all belong to,' '考弊司' is a proper name, and '辖' means 'under the jurisdiction of.'
  • 司主名虚肚鬼王 - '司主' means 'the head of the office,' '名' means 'named,' '虚肚鬼王' is a proper name.
  • 初见之，例应割髀肉，浼君一缓颊耳 - '初见之' means 'at first sight,' '例应' means 'according to custom,' '割髀肉' means 'cut thigh meat,' '浼君' means 'ask you,' and '一缓颊耳' means 'to intercede once.'
  • 不必有罪，此是旧例 - '不必有罪' means 'not necessarily guilty,' and '此是旧例' means 'this is an old custom.'
  • 苦丰于贿者可赎也 - '苦丰于贿' means 'if one is rich in bribes,' and '可赎也' means 'can be redeemed.'
  • 我贫 - '我贫' means 'I am poor.'
  • 我素不稔鬼王，何能效力 - '我素不稔' means 'I have never been acquainted,' '鬼王' is a proper name, and '何能效力' means 'how can I be of any use?'
  • 君前世是伊大父行，宜可听从 - '君前世' means 'in your past life,' '伊大父行' means 'his grandfather,' and '宜可听从' means 'should be able to listen to.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 考弊司 - A fictional or mythical bureaucratic office, possibly a reference to a place where souls are judged or punished.
  • 虚肚鬼王 - A fictional or mythical figure, possibly a deity or judge in the afterlife.
  • 河南 - A province in China, known for its historical significance.
  • 前世 - Refers to a past life, indicating a belief in reincarnation.
  • 大父 - Refers to a grandfather or a respected elder.

Proper Nouns

  • 考弊司 - A fictional or mythical bureaucratic office.
  • 虚肚鬼王 - A fictional or mythical figure.
  • 闻人生 - A proper name.
  • 河南 - A province in China.

[810]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 言次 - Means 'during the conversation' or 'in the course of speaking.'
  • 城郭 - Refers to the city walls and moat, symbolizing the urban environment.
  • 廨宇 - Refers to government offices or administrative buildings.
  • 弘敞 - Means 'spacious' or 'grand.'
  • 考弊司 - Likely refers to an office or department dealing with corruption or inefficiency.
  • 楹間 - Refers to the space between pillars or columns.
  • 板雕翠色 - Describes the carved wooden boards with green color, possibly symbolizing nature or life.
  • 曰校、曰序、曰庠 - Refers to different levels of educational institutions in ancient China.
  • 上士、中士、下士 - Refers to different ranks of officials or scholars.
  • 禮樂鬼門生 - A metaphorical expression, possibly referring to the ghostly nature of the officials or the bureaucratic system.
  • 驟起告别 - Means 'to suddenly rise and take leave.'
  • 侧行送之 - Means 'to walk alongside and see off.'
  • 至堂下 - Means 'to the hall below.'
  • 交臂历指 - Describes the scene where the scholars are being restrained or interrogated.
  • 徽纆 - Refers to ropes or cords used for binding.
  • 裸其股 - Means 'to expose their thighs.'
  • 割片肉 - Means 'to cut a piece of meat.'
  • 骈三指许 - Means 'about the width of three fingers.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 考弊司 - Likely refers to an office or department dealing with corruption or inefficiency in the bureaucracy.
  • 曰校、曰序、曰庠 - These terms refer to different levels of educational institutions in ancient China, reflecting the hierarchical nature of the educational system.
  • 上士、中士、下士 - These terms refer to different ranks of officials or scholars, indicating the social hierarchy within the bureaucracy.
  • 禮樂 - Refers to the rituals and music that were central to Confucian governance and social order.
  • 鬼門生 - A metaphorical expression, possibly referring to the ghostly nature of the officials or the bureaucratic system, suggesting a dark or ominous atmosphere.

Proper Nouns

  • 考弊司 - The name of an office or department dealing with corruption or inefficiency.
  • 曰校、曰序、曰庠 - Names of different levels of educational institutions.
  • 上士、中士、下士 - Ranks of officials or scholars.
  • 鬼王 - The name of the official or a title given to him.
  • 秀才 - A scholar or student.
  • 徽纆 - Ropes or cords used for binding.
  • 狞人 - A fierce or cruel person.

[811]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 大嗥欲嗄 - Describes the scholar's loud and hoarse cry; '大嗥' means 'loud howl,' and '欲嗄' means 'about to be hoarse.'
  • 憤不自持 - Means 'furious and unable to control oneself.'
  • 藍尉蒼蒼 - Refers to the blue sky, symbolizing heaven; '藍尉' means 'blue and clear,' and '蒼蒼' means 'vast and deep.'
  • 控上帝 - Means 'appeal to the Heavenly Emperor' or 'complain to the Supreme Being.'
  • 此輩與阎羅近 - Means 'these people are close to Yama' (the king of the underworld).
  • 呼之或可應耳 - Means 'calling out might get a response.'
  • 殿陛威赫 - Describes the imposing and awe-inspiring appearance of the throne hall; '殿陛' means 'throne hall,' and '威赫' means 'awe-inspiring.'
  • 伏階號屈 - Means 'prostrate oneself on the steps and cry out in distress.'
  • 怜尔夙世攻苦 - Means 'I pity you for your past life of hardship.'
  • 暂委此任 - Means 'temporarily entrusted with this task.'
  • 候生贵家 - Means 'wait until you are born into a noble family.'
  • 去若善筋，增若恶骨 - Means 'remove your good sinews and add bad bones.'
  • 仆地 - Means 'fall to the ground.'
  • 颠落一齿 - Means 'fall and lose a tooth.'
  • 以刀割指端 - Means 'cut the tip of the finger with a knife.'
  • 抽筋出 - Means 'pull out the sinews.'
  • 亮白如丝 - Means 'bright white like silk.'
  • 棰之 - Means 'beat him.'
  • 声类斩豕 - Means 'the sound is like slaughtering a pig.'
  • 手足并抽讫 - Means 'both hands and feet are pulled out completely.'
  • 押去 - Means 'take away.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 秀才 - Refers to a scholar or a person who has passed the imperial examinations.
  • 阎罗 - Refers to Yama, the king of the underworld in Chinese mythology.
  • 上帝 - Refers to the Heavenly Emperor, a term used for the supreme deity in Chinese religion.
  • 殿陛 - Refers to the throne hall, a place of great authority and power.
  • 夙世 - Refers to past lives, a concept in Buddhism and Taoism.
  • 攻苦 - Refers to enduring hardships and suffering.
  • 恶骨 - Refers to bad bones, symbolizing punishment and suffering.

Proper Nouns

  • 秀才 - Scholar.
  • 阎罗 - Yama, the king of the underworld.
  • 上帝 - The Heavenly Emperor.
  • 殿陛 - Throne hall.
  • 恶骨 - Bad bones.

[812]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 稽首 - A formal act of prostration, indicating deep respect or reverence.
  • 感荷殷殷 - Expresses deep gratitude or appreciation.
  • 挽送 - To accompany someone on their way, often with a sense of escorting.
  • 過市 - To pass through the marketplace or town.
  • 垂朱帘 - To hang a red curtain, often used to describe a modest or humble home.
  • 露半面 - To show part of one's face, often implying a shy or reserved demeanor.
  • 容妆绝美 - Describes a woman's extraordinary beauty.
  • 曲巷 - A narrow, winding alley, often associated with the less affluent or secluded areas.
  • 低徊 - To linger or hesitate, often with a sense of reluctance.
  • 坚止 - To firmly insist or stop someone from leaving.
  • 心何忍 - How could one bear to do something, expressing reluctance or pity.
  • 固辞 - To firmly decline or refuse.
  • 顿念 - To suddenly think of or realize something.
  • 腰橐空虚 - To have an empty purse or wallet, indicating poverty or lack of funds.
  • 愧惶 - To feel ashamed and uneasy.
  • 酒阑 - To finish drinking, often implying the end of a meal or gathering.
  • 入帷 - To enter the bedroom or private quarters.
  • 欢爱殊浓 - To have an unusually intense or passionate relationship.
  • 订婚嫁 - To discuss or arrange marriage.
  • 薪水告竭 - To run out of money for basic needs.
  • 耗郎君金资 - To spend the gentleman's money.
  • 奈何 - What to do? Often used to express helplessness or resignation.
  • 暂解衣为质 - To temporarily remove one's clothes as collateral.
  • 呶呶不满志 - To complain or grumble incessantly, expressing dissatisfaction.
  • 入 - To enter, often implying a private or intimate action.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 秀才 - A scholar or student, often used in a respectful sense, reflecting the social status of educated individuals in ancient China.
  • 市 - The marketplace, a central social and economic hub in traditional Chinese towns.
  • 朱帘 - Red curtains, often used to describe a modest or humble home, reflecting the aesthetic and cultural significance of color in Chinese architecture.
  • 曲巷 - Narrow, winding alleys, often associated with the less affluent or secluded areas, reflecting the social and spatial organization of traditional Chinese cities.
  • 入帷 - Entering the bedroom or private quarters, reflecting the cultural norms of privacy and intimacy in ancient China.
  • 订婚嫁 - Discussing or arranging marriage, reflecting the importance of marriage in traditional Chinese society.
  • 薪水 - Basic needs, often used to describe the necessities of life, reflecting the economic realities of the time.
  • 郎君 - A term of address for a gentleman, often used by women, reflecting the social and gender dynamics of the period.
  • 金资 - Money or wealth, reflecting the economic context of the story.

Proper Nouns

  • 秀才 - Scholar or student, a respectful term for an educated individual.
  • 市 - Marketplace, a central social and economic hub in traditional Chinese towns.
  • 朱帘 - Red curtains, often used to describe a modest or humble home.
  • 曲巷 - Narrow, winding alley, often associated with the less affluent or secluded areas.
  • 入帷 - Bedroom or private quarters, reflecting the cultural norms of privacy and intimacy.
  • 订婚嫁 - Marriage, reflecting the importance of marriage in traditional Chinese society.
  • 薪水 - Basic needs, reflecting the economic realities of the time.
  • 郎君 - Gentleman, a term of address for a man, often used by women.
  • 金资 - Money or wealth, reflecting the economic context of the story.

[813]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 生慚 - Literally means 'the student felt ashamed,' indicating a sense of embarrassment or guilt.
  • 移時 - Means 'after a while' or 'for some time,' indicating a passage of time.
  • 猶冀女出展別 - 'Still hoping the girl would come out to say goodbye,' expressing continued hope.
  • 再訂前約 - 'To renew the previous agreement,' indicating a desire to reaffirm a prior commitment.
  • 潛入窺之 - 'Peered in secretly,' suggesting stealthy observation.
  • 目睒睒相對立 - 'Their eyes glaring at each other,' indicating a tense or hostile gaze.
  • 大惧 - 'Great fear,' expressing intense fear.
  • 百道岐出 - 'A hundred paths branching out,' suggesting numerous and confusing directions.
  • 莫知所從 - 'Not knowing which way to go,' indicating confusion or uncertainty.
  • 問之市人 - 'Asked the people in the market,' indicating seeking information from locals.
  • 並無知其村名者 - 'None knew the name of the village,' indicating that the village was unknown or remote.
  • 徘徊廛肆之間 - 'Wandered between shops and stalls,' indicating aimless wandering.
  • 歷兩昏曉 - 'Through two nights and days,' indicating a long period of time.
  • 凄意含酸 - 'Feeling sad and bitter,' expressing a mix of sadness and resentment.
  • 響腸鳴餓 - 'Hunger growling in the stomach,' indicating physical hunger.
  • 進退不能自決 - 'Unable to decide whether to go forward or back,' indicating indecision.
  • 秀才過 - 'Passed by a scholar,' indicating a chance encounter.
  • 驚曰 - 'Exclaimed in surprise,' indicating a sudden reaction.
  • 何尚未歸 - 'Why haven't you returned yet,' indicating a question about the delay.
  • 簡亵若此 - 'So disrespectful,' indicating a lack of proper conduct.
  • 腼顏莫對 - 'Unable to face with embarrassment,' indicating shame or discomfort.
  • 有之矣 - 'Yes, that's right,' indicating agreement.
  • 得毋為花夜叉所迷耶 - 'Could it be that you were bewitched by the Flower Demon,' indicating suspicion of supernatural influence.
  • 盛氣而往 - 'With great anger,' indicating strong emotion.
  • 何遽不少施面目耶 - 'Why not show your face a little,' indicating a demand for confrontation.
  • 去少時 - 'After a short while,' indicating a brief period.
  • 即以衣來付生曰 - 'Immediately brought the clothes to the student and said,' indicating a quick action followed by a statement.
  • 淫婢無禮 - 'The lewd maid was impolite,' indicating criticism of the maid's behavior.
  • 已叱罵之矣 - 'Has already scolded her,' indicating a reprimand.
  • 送生至家 - 'Saw the student home,' indicating a gesture of care.
  • 乃別而去 - 'Then parted ways,' indicating a farewell.
  • 暴絕三日而蘇 - 'Fainted for three days and then revived,' indicating a severe illness.
  • 歷歷為家人言之 - 'Described it in detail to the family,' indicating a recounting of events.
  • 自言夜作阎罗王 - 'Said he was the Lord of Hell at night,' indicating a claim of supernatural experience.
  • 州有馬生亦然 - 'There was also a man named Ma in the state who had the same experience,' indicating a parallel account.
  • 訪諸其家 - 'Visited his family,' indicating a visit to inquire.
  • 問馬昨夕冥中处分何事 - 'Asked Ma what he did in the underworld last night,' indicating a specific inquiry.
  • 無他事 - 'No other matters,' indicating a simple response.
  • 但送左萝石升天 - 'Only sent Left Lotus Rock to heaven,' indicating a specific action.
  • 天上堕莲花 - 'Dropped a lotus flower in heaven,' indicating a supernatural event.
  • 朵大如屋 - 'As large as a house,' indicating the size of the lotus flower.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 生 - Refers to the main character, a student or scholar.
  • 女 - Refers to the girl the student hoped to see.
  • 花夜叉 - A supernatural being, often associated with beauty and danger.
  • 秀才 - Refers to a scholar or literatus, often a respected figure in traditional Chinese society.
  • 阎罗王 - The Lord of Hell, a deity in Chinese mythology responsible for judging the dead.
  • 马生 - A man named Ma, who also had an experience with the Lord of Hell.

  • This sentence describes a sequence of events: the guests panic and flee, then see a light on a hilltop, and finally rush towards it.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 今某生貴矣，相狎者猶以為戲 - Colloquial phrase meaning 'Now that someone has become wealthy, those who were once close to him still regard him as a joke.' Conveys a sense of irony or disdain.
  • 山市 - Literally 'mountain market,' but in this context, it refers to a rare atmospheric phenomenon known as a 'mountain city' or 'fairy city.'
  • 邑八景之一也 - Means 'one of the eight scenic spots of the county,' indicating that this phenomenon is a notable local attraction.
  • 數年恒不一見 - Means 'it is rarely seen once every few years,' emphasizing the rarity of the event.
  • 高插青冥 - Means 'towering into the blue sky,' describing the height of the solitary tower.
  • 相顧驚疑 - Means 'looking at each other in surprise and doubt,' capturing the initial reaction of the observers.
  • 念近中無此禅院 - Means 'thinking there is no such temple nearby,' expressing the observers' confusion.
  • 始悟為山市 - Means 'they finally realized it was a mountain city,' indicating the moment of recognition.
  • 高垣睥睨 - Means 'high walls and battlements,' describing the appearance of the city walls.
  • 居然城郭矣 - Means 'it suddenly became a city,' indicating the transformation of the scene.
  • 历历在目 - Means 'clearly visible,' describing the clarity of the view.
  • 尘气莽莽然 - Means 'the dust is thick and hazy,' describing the atmosphere during the wind.
  • 一切乌有 - Means 'everything disappeared,' indicating the sudden vanishing of the scene.
  • 惟危楼一座 - Means 'only a tall building remained,' describing the final structure.
  • 窗扉皆洞开 - Means 'the windows and doors were all open,' indicating the openness of the building.
  • 一行有五点明处 - Means 'there were five bright points in a row,' describing the lights inside the building.
  • 层层指数 - Means 'counting layer by layer,' indicating the process of observing the building's layers.
  • 裁如星点 - Means 'reduced to the size of stars,' describing the shrinking of the building's appearance.
  • 黯然缥缈 - Means 'dim and hazy,' describing the final appearance of the building.
  • 不一状 - Means 'in various forms,' describing the different postures of the people in the building.
  • 倏忽如拳如豆 - Means 'suddenly shrunk to the size of fists and beans,' describing the rapid shrinking of the building.
  • 遂不可见 - Means 'and then it disappeared,' indicating the final vanishing of the building.
  • 见山上人烟市肆 - Means 'seeing people, smoke, and shops on the mountain,' describing the scene of the early traveler.
  • 与世无别 - Means 'no different from the world,' indicating that the scene was similar to the real world.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 山市 - A rare atmospheric phenomenon that has been described in Chinese literature for centuries, often associated with mystical or supernatural events.
  • 邑八景之一也 - Refers to the tradition of identifying and celebrating the most beautiful or notable sights in a region, a common practice in Chinese culture.
  • 高插青冥 - The phrase '青冥' (blue sky) is often used in Chinese literature to describe the vastness and height of the sky, symbolizing the sublime and the divine.
  • 高垣睥睨 - The term '睥睨' (battlements) is a technical term for the parapets on the walls of a city, indicating the defensive nature of the structure.
  • 居然城郭矣 - The term '城郭' (city walls) refers to the fortified walls of a city, a common feature in Chinese urban planning and defense.
  • 尘气莽莽然 - The phrase '莽莽然' (thick and hazy) is often used to describe the atmosphere during natural phenomena, such as dust storms or fog.
  • 一切乌有 - The term '乌有' (disappeared) is a literary device used to describe the sudden vanishing of a scene, often associated with magical or supernatural events.
  • 惟危楼一座 - The term '危楼' (tall building) is often used in Chinese literature to describe structures that are both impressive and awe-inspiring.
  • 窗扉皆洞开 - The term '洞开' (open) indicates the openness and transparency of the building, suggesting a sense of freedom or exposure.
  • 一行有五点明处 - The term '明处' (bright points) is often used to describe lights or sources of illumination, indicating the presence of activity or life within the building.
  • 层层指数 - The term '指数' (counting) is a technical term used to describe the process of measuring or quantifying something, in this case, the layers of the building.
  • 裁如星点 - The term '星点' (star points) is a metaphorical expression used to describe small, distant objects, often associated with the night sky.
  • 黯然缥缈 - The term '缥缈' (dim and hazy) is often used to describe the appearance of distant or fading objects, suggesting a sense of mystery or unreality.
  • 不一状 - The term '不一状' (in various forms) is a descriptive phrase used to indicate diversity or variety, in this case, the different postures of the people in the building.
  • 倏忽如拳如豆 - The term '倏忽' (suddenly) is a temporal adverb used to describe rapid changes, often associated with the fleeting nature of phenomena.
  • 遂不可见 - The term '遂不可见' (and then it disappeared) is a literary device used to describe the final vanishing of a scene, often associated with magical or supernatural events.
  • 见山上人烟市肆 - The term '人烟市肆' (people, smoke, and shops) is a descriptive phrase used to indicate the presence of human activity and commerce, suggesting a bustling and lively scene.
  • 与世无别 - The term '无别' (no different) is a comparative phrase used to indicate similarity or equivalence, in this case, the similarity between the mountain scene and the real world.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '今某生贵矣，相狎者犹以为戏' is elliptical and grammatically compact. A more complete version would be: 'Now that someone has become wealthy, those who were once close to him still regard him as a joke.'
  • The sentence '山市，邑八景之一也，数年恒不一见' is elliptical and grammatically compact. A more complete version would be: 'The mountain city, one of the eight scenic spots of the county, is rarely seen once every few years.'
  • The sentence '孙公子禹年，与同人饮楼上，忽见山头有孤塔耸起，高插青冥' is elliptical and grammatically compact. A more complete version would be: 'Mr. Sun, a gentleman named Yu, was drinking with his companions on the upper floor of a building when he suddenly saw a solitary tower rise from the mountain, towering into the blue sky.'
  • The sentence '相顾惊疑，念近中无此禅院' is elliptical and grammatically compact. A more complete version would be: 'They looked at each other in surprise and doubt, thinking there is no such temple nearby.'
  • The sentence '始悟为山市' is elliptical and grammatically compact. A more complete version would be: 'They finally realized it was a mountain city.'
  • The sentence '未几高垣睥睨，连亘六七里，居然城郭矣' is elliptical and grammatically compact. A more complete version would be: 'Soon after, high walls and battlements appeared, stretching for six or seven miles, and it suddenly became a city.'
  • The sentence '中有楼若者、堂若者、坊若者，历历在目，以亿万计' is elliptical and grammatically compact. A more complete version would be: 'Inside, there were buildings like towers, halls, and marketplaces, clearly visible, numbering in the millions.'
  • The sentence '忽大风起，尘气莽莽然，城市依稀而已' is elliptical and grammatically compact. A more complete version would be: 'Suddenly, a strong wind arose, and the dust became thick and hazy, and the city became faintly visible.'
  • The sentence '既而风定天清，一切乌有；惟危楼一座，直接霄汉' is elliptical and grammatically compact. A more complete version would be: 'After the wind subsided and the sky cleared, everything disappeared; only a tall building remained, reaching directly into the heavens.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 彼愛之，我獨何仇乎？ - Rhetorical question expressing confusion or frustration. Literally: 'He loves her, why do I alone have a grudge against her?'
  • 高呼子而罵 - 'Shouted at his son and cursed him.' *高呼* means to shout or call out loudly, and *罵* is to curse or scold.
  • 生但俯首，不少出气 - 'The son merely lowered his head, not uttering a word.' *但* means 'only,' *俯首* is to lower one's head in submission or silence, *不少出气* is a phrase meaning 'not even exhaling' or 'not uttering a single word.'
  • 言間，樊已送女至 - 'Before the words had finished, Fan had already brought the daughter.' *言間* is an idiom meaning 'in the interval of speaking' or 'before the words were fully spoken.'
  • 析爨之盟 - 'The agreement to separate the hearth.' *析爨* is a classical expression meaning to divide the cooking hearth, symbolizing the separation of families or households.
  • 各立门户 - 'To establish separate households.' A common phrase meaning to live separately, often used in the context of divorce or family conflict.
  • 即烦主析爨之盟 - 'I will request the master to formalize the agreement to separate the hearth.' *即烦主* is a polite way of saying 'I will request the master.'
  • 颇相安 - 'They were relatively at peace.' *颇* means 'somewhat' or 'fairly,' and *相安* means 'to live in peace with each other.'
  • 翁妪窃慰 - 'The old man and old woman secretly felt relieved.' *窃慰* is a humble expression for 'secretly comforted' or 'secretly consoled.'
  • 未几女渐肆 - 'Not long after, the daughter gradually became arrogant.' *未几* is a classical time phrase meaning 'not long after' or 'soon after.' *渐肆* means 'gradually becoming unrestrained' or 'gradually becoming insolent.'
  • 生面上时有指爪痕 - 'The son's face would often bear marks of fingernails.' *指爪痕* is a literal term for 'fingernail marks.'
  • 父母明知之，亦忍不置问 - 'The parents knew of it, but they忍 (endured) and did not inquire.' *忍不置问* is a complex expression meaning 'to endure and not question.'
  • 不堪挞楚 - 'Could not bear the beatings.' *挞楚* is an archaic term for 'beatings' or 'whippings.'
  • 芒芒然如鸟雀之被鹯殴者 - 'Helpless and confused, like a bird being attacked by a hawk.' *芒芒然* is an adverb meaning 'helpless' or 'bewildered.' *被鹯殴* is a metaphor for being attacked by a predator.
  • 翁媪方怪问 - 'The old man and old woman were just about to question.' *方怪问* is a phrase meaning 'were just about to question in surprise.'
  • 横梃追入 - 'Ran in with a wooden staff.' *横梃* is a term for a wooden staff or club used in beating.
  • 竟即翁侧捉而棰之 - 'She grabbed him on the side of the old man and beat him.' *竟即* is a conjunction meaning 'then' or 'immediately.' *捉而棰之* is 'grabbed and beat him.'
  • 涕噪 - 'Wept and shouted.' *涕* is tears, *噪* is shouting.
  • 略不顾赡 - 'Paid no heed to the old man or old woman.' *赡* is a term for supporting or caring for elders, but here it is used in the sense of 'taking care of' or 'considering.'
  • 挞至数十 - 'Beaten dozens of times.' *挞* is to beat or whip, *数十* is 'dozens.'
  • 悻悻以去 - 'Left in anger.' *悻悻* is a term for being resentful or angry.
  • 避嚣 - 'To avoid noise.' *嚣* is noise or disturbance.
  • 析尔 - 'Split you off' or 'separated you.' *析* is to divide, *尔* is 'you.'
  • 尔固乐此，又焉逃乎？ - 'You have always enjoyed this, so why do you now want to escape?' *固乐此* is 'have always enjoyed this,' *焉逃* is 'why escape.'
  • 徙倚无所归 - 'Wandered without a place to return to.' *徙倚* is to wander or linger, *无所归* is 'nowhere to go.'
  • 母恐其折挫行死 - 'The mother feared that he would be crushed and die.' *折挫* is to be crushed or broken, *行死* is to die in the act.
  • 独居而给之食 - 'Allowed him to live alone and provided him with food.' *独居* is to live alone, *给之食* is to provide food.
  • 使教其女 - 'To have him teach her daughter.' *使* is to send or have someone do something, *教其女* is 'teach her daughter.'
  • 开谕万端 - 'Spoke at length and tried to reason with her.' *开谕* is to explain or advise, *万端* is 'many ways' or 'various arguments.'
  • 反以恶言相苦 - 'Instead, she spoke to him with harsh words.' *反* is 'instead,' *恶言* is 'harsh words,' *相苦* is 'to torment each other.'
  • 拂衣去 - 'Wiped his robe and left.' *拂衣* is a gesture of leaving in anger or disdain.
  • 誓相绝 - 'Swore to cut off all relations.' *誓* is to swear, *相绝* is 'to cut off each other.'
  • 愤生病 - 'Died of anger or illness.' *愤生* is 'died from anger.'
  • 相继死 - 'Died one after another.' *相继* is 'in succession.'
  • 惟日隔壁噪骂 - 'Only daily shouted and cursed from the other side of the wall.' *惟日* is 'only daily,' *隔壁噪骂* is 'shouted and cursed from the next room.'
  • 高悉置不知 - 'Gao completely ignored it.' *悉置* is 'completely set aside' or 'ignored entirely,' *不知* is 'not knowing.'

  • 近诘之，肆主马姓者便云 - Means 'approached to ask them, the shop owner named Ma said,' where '近诘之' indicates a close inquiry.
  • 伊夫妇饿将死，日向我讨麻酱以为活 - Means 'his wife and husband are starving to death, they come to me daily asking for sesame paste to live,' where '伊' is a respectful way to refer to someone.
  • 今又欲卖妇于我，我家中已买十余口矣 - Means 'now they want to sell their wife to me, I have already bought over ten mouths,' where '十余口' refers to people.
  • 此何要紧？贱则售之，否则已耳 - Means 'what's the big deal? If cheap, sell; otherwise, forget it,' where '何要紧' indicates a dismissive attitude.
  • 如此可笑，生来缠人 - Means 'it's so laughable, they come to bother me,' where '生来缠人' suggests persistent annoyance.
  • 今粟如珠 - Means 'now millet is as precious as pearls,' indicating extreme scarcity.
  • 自度非得三百数，不足供逃亡之费 - Means 'I estimate that without three hundred, it's not enough to escape,' where '自度' implies personal estimation.
  • 本欲两生，若卖妻而不免于死，何敢焉 - Means 'we originally wanted to both survive, if selling the wife still leads to death, how dare we,' where '两生' implies mutual survival.

  • 刘怜之，便问马出几何 - Means 'Liu felt sorry for them and asked Ma how much,' where '怜之' indicates sympathy.

  • 刘请勿短其数，且愿助以半价之资 - Means 'Liu asked not to undervalue it and offered to contribute half the price,' where '短其数' implies undervaluation.
  • 马执不可 - Means 'Ma insisted it was not possible,' where '执不可' indicates firm refusal.
  • 刘少负气，便谓男子 - Means 'Liu, who had a bit of pride, told the man,' where '少负气' indicates a slight sense of pride.
  • 彼鄙琐不足道，我请如数相赠 - Means 'those petty things are not worth mentioning, I will give you the full amount,' where '鄙琐不足道' indicates triviality.

  • 粟如珠 - Indicates the extreme scarcity of millet, a staple food, reflecting the famine conditions.
  • 逃亡 - Refers to fleeing or escaping, indicating the desperate measures taken during the famine.
  • 阴骘 - Refers to unseen virtue or good deeds that bring unseen benefits, a concept from Chinese moral philosophy.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 金便呼媒媪来 - Jin then called the matchmaker (媒媪) to come. *媒媪* is a compound term referring to a female matchmaker or媒人 (matchmaker), with *媪* indicating an older woman. *便呼* is a colloquial expression meaning 'then called' or 'immediately called.'
  • 嘱为物色佳媵 - Instructed her to find a good concubine (佳媵). *物色* means 'to search for' or 'to look for.' *佳媵* is a term for a beautiful or suitable concubine or妾 (concubine).
  • 而阴使迁延勿报 - But secretly instructed her to delay and not report. *阴使* means 'secretly ordered' or 'in secret.' *迁延* is a classical term meaning 'to delay' or 'to procrastinate.' *勿报* is 'not to report' or 'not to inform.'
  • 己则故督促之 - While he himself deliberately urged her. *故* here means 'intentionally' or 'deliberately.' *督促* means 'to urge' or 'to press.'
  • 如是年余 - Thus, for over a year. *如是* is a classical way of saying 'thus' or 'in this manner.' *年余* is 'over a year.'
  • 柴不能待 - Chai could not wait. *不能待* is 'could not wait' or 'was unable to wait.'
  • 遍嘱戚好为之购致 - He repeatedly instructed his relatives and friends to purchase one for him. *戚好* refers to close relatives and friends. *购致* means 'to purchase and bring (someone).' *为之* is 'for him.'
  • 得林氏之养女 - He obtained the adopted daughter of the Lin family. *养女* is a term for an adopted daughter or a daughter raised by someone else.
  • 金一见，喜形于色 - Jin saw her and showed his joy on his face. *喜形于色* is an idiomatic expression meaning 'to show one's joy on one's face.'
  • 饮食共之 - Shared meals with her. *共之* is a classical way of saying 'shared with her.'
  • 脂泽花钏任其所取 - Allowed her to take whatever cosmetics, ointments, and jewelry she wanted. *脂泽* refers to cosmetics or oils. *花钏* is 'flower bracelets' or 'ornamental bracelets.' *任其所取* is 'allowed her to take whatever she wanted.'
  • 林固燕产，不习女红 - Lin was originally from Yan and was not skilled in needlework. *固* here means 'originally' or 'indeed.' *燕产* is 'from Yan' (Yan was a region in ancient China, now part of Hebei province). *不习女红* means 'not skilled in women's work' or 'not familiar with needlework.'
  • 绣履之外须人而成 - Except for embroidered shoes, everything else required help to complete. *绣履* is 'embroidered shoes.' *须人而成* is 'required help from others to complete.'
  • 我素勤俭，非似王侯家，买作画图看者 - I have always been frugal and thrifty, not like a noble family who buys women to look at like paintings. *素勤俭* means 'always frugal and thrifty.' *非似王侯家* is 'not like a noble family.' *买作画图看者* is a metaphor for treating women like paintings or decorations to be admired.
  • 授美锦，使学制，若严师诲弟子 - He gave her fine silk and made her learn to make shoes, as a strict teacher teaches a disciple. *授美锦* is 'gave her fine silk.' *使学制* is 'made her learn to make (shoes).' *若严师诲弟子* is 'as a strict teacher teaches a disciple.'
  • 初犹呵骂，继而鞭楚 - At first he scolded and cursed her, then later beat her. *呵骂* is 'to scold and curse.' *鞭楚* is 'to beat with a whip or rod.'
  • 柴痛切于心，不能为地 - Chai was deeply pained in his heart and could not stand for it. *痛切于心* is 'deeply pained in the heart.' *不能为地* is 'could not bear it' or 'could not stand for it.'
  • 而金之怜爱林尤倍于昔 - But Jin's affection for Lin was even greater than before. *尤倍于昔* is 'even more than before.'
  • 往往自为汝束，匀铅黄焉 - Often he would braid her hair himself and apply makeup for her. *自为汝束* is 'he would braid your hair himself.' *匀铅黄* is 'apply makeup' (lead and yellow pigments were used in ancient makeup).
  • 但履跟稍有折痕，则以铁杖击双弯 - But if the soles of her shoes had any creases, he would beat her with an iron rod on her arms. *履跟* is 'the soles of the shoes.' *折痕* is 'creases' or 'folds.' *铁杖* is 'iron rod' or 'iron staff.' *击双弯* is 'strike both arms' or 'beat the arms.'
  • 发少乱则批两颊 - If her hair was slightly disheveled, he would strike her cheeks. *发少乱* is 'hair slightly disheveled.' *批两颊* is 'strike both cheeks.'
  • 林不堪其虐，自经死 - Lin could not endure such cruelty and committed suicide by hanging. *不堪其虐* is 'could not endure the cruelty.' *自经死* is 'committed suicide by hanging.'
  • 柴悲惨心目，颇致怨怼 - Chai was filled with sorrow and anger. *悲惨心目* is 'filled with sorrow in his heart and eyes.' *颇致怨怼* is 'began to harbor resentment.'
  • 柴始悟其奸，因复反目，永绝琴瑟之好 - Chai then realized her treachery, and so they turned against each other, forever cutting off their marital harmony. *始悟其奸* is 'realized her treachery.' *复反目* is 'turned against each other.' *永绝琴瑟之好* is 'forever cut off marital harmony' (琴瑟 is a metaphor for marriage or harmonious relationship).
  • 阴于别业修房闼，思购丽人而别居之 - Secretly, he began to prepare a house in a separate estate, intending to buy a beautiful woman and live apart from her. *阴于别业修房闼* is 'secretly prepared a house in a different estate.' *思购丽人而别居之* is 'intended to buy a beautiful woman and live separately from her.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 媒媪 - A traditional matchmaker, typically an older woman, involved in arranging marriages in ancient China.
  • 媵 - A concubine or a wife, often given as a gift or in marriage. In this context, it refers to a secondary wife or妾.
  • 燕产 - Yan was a state in ancient China, located in what is now Hebei Province. *燕产* means 'born in Yan' or 'from Yan.'
  • 女红 - Refers to women's domestic work, especially sewing, embroidery, and other needlework.
  • 绣履 - Embroidered shoes, a symbol of refinement and status in ancient China.
  • 王侯家 - Refers to the nobility or aristocracy, particularly those of high rank like kings or dukes.
  • 买作画图看者 - A metaphor for treating women as objects of beauty or decoration, like paintings.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 一日柴他往 - Narrative phrase indicating 'one day, Chai went elsewhere.' The structure is elliptical, with the subject '柴' (Chai) implied.

  • 命苍头控老牝马 - 'Ordered the servant to control the old mare.' '苍头' refers to a male servant or retainer. '控' means to control or manage, often used in the context of riding a horse. '老牝马' is 'an old female horse' or 'an old mare.'
  • 一妪携襆从之 - 'An old woman carried a bundle and followed her.' '妪' is an old woman; '襆' is a bundle or pack.

  • 伏地而陈 - 'Fell to the ground and presented her case.' '伏地' is to fall to the ground in submission, often used in the context of kneeling or prostration. '陈' means to present or state something.

  • 容饰兼卑 - 'Her appearance and attire were both humble.' '容' is appearance or face; '饰' is adornment or dress.
  • 气亦稍平 - 'Her anger was somewhat calmed.' '气' is anger or temper; '稍平' is slightly calmed.
  • 命婢子出锦衣衣之 - 'Ordered the maid to take out a silk robe and dress her.' '婢子' is a female servant or maid; '锦衣' is a silk robe; '衣之' is to dress her.
  • 彼薄幸人播恶于众 - 'That unfaithful person spread evil among the people.' '薄幸' is unfaithful or untrustworthy; '播恶' is to spread evil or malice.
  • 使我横被口语 - 'Caused me to be slandered.' '横被' is 'suffered unjustly'; '口语' is slander or malicious talk.
  • 诸婢无行 - 'The maids had no virtue.' '无行' is lacking in virtue or morality.
  • 有以激之 - 'Which caused her to act in this way.' '有以' is 'there was a reason'; '激之' is to provoke or incite.
  • 汝试念背妻而立家室 - 'You try to imagine setting up a household while betraying your wife.' '念' is to imagine or consider; '背妻' is to betray one's wife; '立家室' is to establish a household.
  • 此岂复是人矣 - 'Is this still a human being?' A rhetorical question expressing moral condemnation.
  • 细察渠似稍悔之 - 'Upon careful examination, he seems to have slightly regretted it.' '细察' is to examine carefully; '渠' is a pronoun for 'he'; '悔之' is to regret it.
  • 大者不伏小 - 'The greater does not submit to the lesser.' A proverb or saying suggesting that the powerful or higher-ranking person will not yield to the lower.
  • 妻之于夫，犹子之于父，庶之于嫡也 - 'A wife's relationship to her husband is like a son's to his father, or a concubine's to the principal wife.' '犹...之于...' is a simile structure.
  • 夫人若肯假以词色 - 'If you, madam, would show her words and expressions of favor.' '假以词色' is to show words and expressions, i.e., to show kindness or favor.
  • 积怨可以尽捐 - 'The accumulated grievances can be completely abandoned.' '积怨' is accumulated grievances; '尽捐' is to completely abandon or release.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 柴 - Likely a surname; the character '柴' can also mean firewood, but in this context, it is used as a name.
  • 嫡 - Refers to the legitimate wife or the principal wife in a polygamous household, often in contrast to a concubine (庶).
  • 庶 - Refers to a concubine or secondary wife.
  • 婢子 - A female servant or maid, often used in classical texts to refer to lower-ranking female attendants.
  • 锦衣 - A fine silk robe, often associated with status or reward.
  • 薄幸 - A term used to describe someone unfaithful or untrustworthy, often in the context of romantic or marital infidelity.
  • 口语 - Slander or malicious talk, often used in the context of social or moral condemnation.
  • 无行 - Lacking virtue or morality, often used to describe someone who has acted improperly.
  • 大者不伏小 - A proverb or saying suggesting that the higher or more powerful will not submit to the lower, possibly referencing social or hierarchical relations.
  • 妻之于夫，犹子之于父，庶之于嫡也 - A classical analogy used to establish the hierarchy of relationships in a traditional Chinese family structure, emphasizing the subordination of the wife to the husband, the son to the father, and the concubine to the principal wife.

  • 晓妆犹含盛怒 - 'Dawn makeup still holding intense anger,' indicating that the wife's anger persists even as she prepares for the day.
  • 女捧镜，镜堕，破之 - 'The girl holds the mirror, which falls and breaks,' a literal description of an event that triggers further conflict.
  • 妻益恚，握发裂眦 - 'The wife becomes even angrier, gripping her hair and glaring,' vividly depicting her emotional state.
  • 女惧，长跪哀免 - 'The girl, fearing, kneels and pleads for forgiveness,' showing her attempt to appease the wife.
  • 怒不解，鞭之至数十 - 'The anger does not subside, and she beats her dozens of times,' indicating the severity of the wife's punishment.
  • 柴不能忍，盛气奔入，曳女出 - 'Chai cannot bear it any longer, rushes in with great anger, and drags the girl out,' showing Chai's intervention.
  • 妻呶呶逐击之 - 'The wife continues to shout and hit her,' describing her relentless aggression.
  • 柴怒，夺鞭反扑，面肤绽裂，始退 - 'Chai, enraged, snatches the whip and retaliates, causing her face to split open, and she finally retreats,' detailing the physical confrontation.
  • 由是夫妻若仇 - 'From then on, the couple became like enemies,' summarizing the deterioration of their relationship.
  • 柴禁女无往，女弗听，早起，膝行伺幕外 - 'Chai forbids the girl from going anywhere, but she does not listen, gets up early, and crawls outside the screen,' showing the girl's defiance.
  • 妻捶床怒骂，叱去，不听前 - 'The wife slams the bed and scolds, ordering her away, but she does not listen,' depicting the ongoing conflict.
  • 日夜切齿，将伺柴出而后泄愤于女 - 'She gnashes her teeth day and night, planning to wait for Chai to leave and then vent her anger on the girl,' revealing her vengeful intentions.
  • 柴知之，谢绝人事，杜门不通吊庆 - 'Chai knows this and refuses social visits, closing the door to all condolences and celebrations,' showing his isolation.
  • 妻无如何，惟日挞婢媪以寄其恨，下人皆不可堪 - 'The wife, unable to do anything, only beats the maids and servants to vent her anger, and the servants cannot bear it,' describing her frustration.
  • 自夫妻绝好，女亦莫敢当夕，柴于是孤眠 - 'Since the couple stopped being intimate, the girl dares not share the bed, and Chai sleeps alone,' highlighting the emotional distance.
  • 妻闻之，意不稍安，有大婢索狡黠，偶与柴语，妻疑其私，暴之尤苦 - 'The wife, hearing this, feels uneasy, and a clever maid named Suo occasionally speaks with Chai, leading the wife to suspect an affair and treat her especially harshly,' revealing her jealousy and suspicion.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 自病后，皆散诞无操作者 - After falling ill, they all became lax and did not work.

  • 金问故，始实告之 - Gold (the master) asked why, and she finally told him the truth.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 疑曰 - A formal way of introducing dialogue, meaning 'Doubtful One said.'
  • 技止此类 - Literally 'skill only of this kind,' meaning that the skill is limited to this type of treatment.
  • 病本何以不拔 - Literally 'how is the root of the illness not removed,' questioning why the root cause of the illness is not addressed.
  • 请再灼之 - Literally 'please burn again,' referring to the practice of cauterization.
  • 非烂烧不可 - Literally 'cannot be burned thoroughly,' meaning that a severe burn is necessary.
  • 恐夫人不能忍受 - Literally 'fear that the lady cannot bear it,' expressing concern about the patient's ability to endure the pain.
  • 忆梦中语 - Literally 'remembered the words in the dream,' referring to a dream that was recalled.
  • 无难色 - Literally 'no difficulty in expression,' meaning that the patient showed no signs of distress.
  • 呻吟忍受 - Literally 'groan and endure,' describing the patient's reaction to the pain.
  • 默思欠此十九针 - Literally 'silently think about lacking these nineteen needles,' indicating a reflection on the missing nineteen needles.
  • 不知作何变症 - Literally 'do not know what kind of complications will arise,' expressing uncertainty about potential side effects.
  • 不如一朝受尽 - Literally 'it is better to endure it all at once,' suggesting that it is better to face the pain all at once rather than in parts.
  • 庶免后苦 - Literally 'may avoid later suffering,' hoping to prevent future pain.
  • 炷尽 - Literally 'the incense stick is finished,' referring to the completion of the cauterization process.
  • 求女再针 - Literally 'ask the woman to needle again,' requesting another round of acupuncture.
  • 针岂可以泛常施用耶 - Literally 'can needles be used casually?' questioning the appropriateness of using needles indiscriminately.
  • 不必论穴 - Literally 'no need to discuss the points,' suggesting that the specific acupuncture points are not important.
  • 但烦十九刺 - Literally 'just trouble with nineteen pricks,' requesting nineteen acupuncture treatments.
  • 笑不可 - Literally 'laugh and say no,' indicating that the request was refused with a laugh.
  • 请益坚 - Literally 'plead more firmly,' meaning that the request was made more insistently.
  • 起跪榻上 - Literally 'rise and kneel on the bed,' describing the patient's posture.
  • 终不忍 - Literally 'ultimately cannot bear,' indicating that the woman could not refuse the request.
  • 实以梦告 - Literally 'truthfully tell about the dream,' referring to the explanation of the dream.
  • 约略经络刺之如数 - Literally 'roughly according to the meridians, prick as many as the number,' describing the acupuncture treatment based on the dream.
  • 自此平复 - Literally 'from this point on, recover,' indicating that the patient recovered.
  • 果不复病 - Literally 'indeed no longer sick,' confirming that the patient did not fall ill again.
  • 弥自忏悔 - Literally 'more and more repent,' indicating a growing sense of remorse.
  • 临下亦无戾色 - Literally 'when facing subordinates, also without anger,' showing that the patient was no longer angry.
  • 子名曰俊 - Literally 'the son is named Jun,' introducing the son.
  • 秀惠绝伦 - Literally 'exceedingly talented and wise,' describing the son's exceptional qualities.
  • 此子翰苑相也 - Literally 'this son is destined for the Hanlin Academy,' indicating that the son is destined for a prestigious position.
  • 八岁有神童之目 - Literally 'at eight years old, considered a prodigy,' describing the son's early recognition as a prodigy.
  • 十五岁以进士授翰林 - Literally 'at fifteen years old, granted the Hanlin position through the imperial examination,' describing the son's early success in the imperial examination.
  • 是时柴夫妇年四十 - Literally 'at that time, the couple was forty years old,' providing the age of the couple.
  • 如夫人三十有二三耳 - Literally 'the concubine was around thirty-two or thirty-three,' providing the age of the concubine.
  • 舆马归宁 - Literally 'return home in a carriage,' describing the return home in a carriage.
  • 乡里荣之 - Literally 'the village is proud of it,' indicating that the village is proud of the son's success.
  • 邵翁自鬻女后 - Literally 'after selling the daughter, the old man,' referring to the old man after selling his daughter.
  • 家暴富 - Literally 'the family became suddenly wealthy,' indicating a sudden increase in wealth.
  • 而士林羞与为伍 - Literally 'but the scholarly world is ashamed to associate with him,' indicating that the scholarly world is ashamed to associate with the old man.
  • 至是始有通往来者 - Literally 'from this point on, there are those who communicate with him,' indicating that communication with the old man began.

  • 尝求见鲁王 - Means 'once sought to meet the Prince of Lu,' indicating an attempt to gain an audience with a noble.
  • 阍人不为通 - Means 'the gatekeeper did not allow him to pass,' showing that Gong was initially denied entry.
  • 中贵人出，揖求之 - Means 'a high-ranking official came out and bowed to him, seeking him,' indicating that a powerful figure attempted to engage with Gong.
  • 中贵见其鄙陋，逐去之 - Means 'the high-ranking official saw him as crude and unrefined, and drove him away,' showing that Gong was rejected due to his appearance.
  • 已而复来 - Means 'and then came back again,' indicating that Gong persisted in his attempt to gain entry.
  • 中贵怒，且逐且扑 - Means 'the high-ranking official became angry and chased him while hitting him,' showing that Gong was physically assaulted.
  • 至无人处，道人笑出黄金二百两 - Means 'when he reached a place where no one was around, the Daoist practitioner laughed and produced two hundred taels of gold,' indicating a surprising display of wealth.
  • 烦逐者覆中贵 - Means 'he asked the one who was chasing him to deliver a message to the high-ranking official,' showing that Gong used the messenger to communicate indirectly.
  • 为言我亦不要见王 - Means 'to say that I also do not want to see the Prince,' indicating that Gong did not actually wish to meet the Prince.
  • 但闻后苑花木楼台，极人间佳胜 - Means 'but I have heard that the gardens, flowers, trees, and towers in the rear garden are the finest in the world,' showing that Gong was more interested in the scenery.
  • 若能导我一游，生平足矣 - Means 'if you can guide me through it, it will be enough for my life,' indicating that Gong was willing to pay for the experience.
  • 又以白金赂逐者 - Means 'he also bribed the one who was chasing him with silver,' showing that Gong used bribery to gain favor.
  • 其人喜，反命 - Means 'the person was pleased and returned to report,' indicating that the messenger was satisfied with the bribe.
  • 中贵亦喜，引道人自后宰门入 - Means 'the high-ranking official was also pleased and led the Daoist practitioner into the rear gate,' showing that Gong was finally allowed to enter.
  • 诸景俱历 - Means 'he experienced all the sights,' indicating that Gong saw everything in the garden.
  • 又从登楼上，中贵方凭窗 - Means 'and then he climbed up to a tower, where the high-ranking official was leaning against a window,' showing that Gong ascended to a higher vantage point.
  • 道人一推，但觉身堕楼外 - Means 'when the Daoist practitioner pushed, he felt himself falling out of the tower,' indicating that Gong caused the official to fall.
  • 有细葛绷腰，悬于空际 - Means 'a fine hemp rope was tied around his waist, suspending him in mid-air,' showing that Gong was somehow suspended without falling to his death.
  • 下视则高深晕目，葛隐隐作断声 - Means 'when he looked down, the height was dizzying, and the hemp rope seemed to be breaking,' indicating that Gong was in a precarious position.
  • 惧极，大号 - Means 'he was terrified and shouted loudly,' showing that Gong was in distress.
  • 无何数监至，骇极 - Means 'soon several attendants arrived, greatly frightened,' indicating that help came quickly.
  • 见其去地绝远，登楼共视 - Means 'they saw that he was far from the ground, and they all climbed up to look,' showing that the attendants were concerned for Gong's safety.
  • 则葛端系根上，欲解援之，则葛细不堪用力 - Means 'the hemp rope was tied to a root, and when they tried to untie it, the rope was too thin to support their weight,' indicating that the rope was fragile.
  • 遍索道人，已杳矣 - Means 'they searched everywhere for the Daoist practitioner, but he had disappeared,' showing that Gong had vanished.
  • 束手无计，奏之鲁王 - Means 'they were at a loss and reported it to the Prince of Lu,' indicating that they sought help from the Prince.
  • 王诣视大奇之 - Means 'the Prince came to see and was greatly surprised,' showing that the Prince was amazed by the incident.
  • 命楼下藉茅铺絮，将因而断之 - Means 'he ordered that straw and cotton be laid below to cushion the fall, and then he ordered the rope to be cut,' indicating that the Prince took precautions.
  • 甫毕，葛崩然自绝 - Means 'as soon as it was done, the hemp rope suddenly broke,' showing that the rope snapped.
  • 去地乃不咫耳 - Means 'he was only a short distance from the ground,' indicating that Gong was saved from falling.
  • 相与失笑 - Means 'they all laughed together,' showing that the situation was resolved humorously.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '大商虽被创，而金帛无所亡失' uses a concessive structure to indicate that despite the merchant's hardship, his wealth remained intact.

  • The phrase '久之寂不闻' indicates a period of silence or lack of communication, which is crucial for understanding the timeline of events.
  • The clause '贫馁愈不可支' describes the worsening situation, emphasizing the urgency of the problem.
  • The suggestion '不如鬻宅于兄' is a practical solution, reflecting the merchant's pragmatic approach to solving his problems.
  • The clause '不如反其券而周之' is a generous and compassionate act, showing the elder brother's kindness.
  • The final clause '不如受其券，从所适，亦可以广吾宅' combines pragmatism with a sense of fairness, allowing the younger brother to move on while the elder brother benefits.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 無何女至 - 'Soon after, the woman arrived.' '無何' is a classical expression meaning 'not long after' or 'soon.'
  • 举止皆佳 - 'Her conduct and manner were all excellent.' '举止' refers to behavior and deportment.
  • 因大惊异 - 'He was greatly surprised.' '因' here introduces the consequence of the previous action.
  • 女但掩口微笑 - 'The woman merely covered her mouth and smiled.' '但' means 'only' or 'merely.'
  • 展细诘之 - 'Zhan questioned her in detail.' '诘' means to interrogate or question.
  • 女进退而惭于言 - 'The woman was hesitant and embarrassed in her speech.' '进退' here refers to hesitation or uncertainty in speaking.
  • 封为略述梗概 - 'Feng briefly recounted the general outline.' '梗概' means the main points or summary.

  • 使子大成与婿同学 - 'He had his son Dacheng study together with the son-in-law.' '与...同学' is a classical expression for 'study together with.'

  • 因而郎舅不相能 - 'Thus, the husband and father-in-law could no longer get along.' '不相能' means to be unable to get along or to have strained relations.
  • 厮仆亦刻疵其短 - 'Even the servants and attendants criticized her shortcomings.' '刻疵' means to point out flaws or criticize.
  • 展惑于浸润 - 'Zhan was swayed by the gradual influence.' '浸润' refers to a slow, insidious influence or persuasion.
  • 礼稍懈 - 'His respect and courtesy became somewhat relaxed.' '礼' here refers to proper conduct or respect.
  • 女觉之 - 'The woman realized this.' '觉' means to realize or become aware.
  • 岳家不可久居 - 'It is not advisable to stay in the husband’s family for long.' '岳家' refers to the husband’s family (the in-laws).
  • 凡久居者，尽阘茸也 - 'All those who stay long are of low character.' '阘茸' is a rare term meaning lowly or base people.
  • 及今未大决裂，宜速归 - 'Now that it has not yet reached a complete rupture, it would be best to return quickly.' '及今' is a classical expression for 'now that' or 'before it is too late.'
  • 封然之 - 'Feng agreed with her.' '然' is a classical particle indicating agreement.
  • 告展 - 'He told Zhan.' '告' means to inform or tell.
  • 女不可 - 'The woman refused.' '不可' is a negative response.
  • 父兄尽怒 - 'Her father and brothers were all angry.' '尽' means 'all' or 'completely.'
  • 不给舆马 - 'They did not provide a carriage or horse.' '舆马' refers to transportation.
  • 女自出妆资贳马归 - 'The woman herself used her dowry to hire a horse and returned.' '贳' means to borrow or hire (in this context, likely 'hire' or 'buy with her own funds').
  • 后展招令归宁 - 'Later, Zhan invited her to return home for a visit.' '归宁' is a classical term for a daughter returning to her parents' home for a visit.
  • 女固辞不往 - 'The woman firmly refused to go.' '固辞' means to refuse firmly or decisively.
  • 后封举孝廉 - 'Later, Feng was recommended as a *xiaolian*.' '孝廉' is a Han dynasty official examination system where candidates were recommended based on virtue and talent.
  • 始通庆好 - 'They finally resumed friendly relations.' '通庆好' means to reestablish good relations or to reconcile.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 孝廉 - A Han dynasty system of recommending candidates for official positions based on moral virtue and talent. This is a key bureaucratic term in the Han period.
  • 归宁 - A traditional term referring to a married daughter returning to her parents' home for a visit, often for festivals or important family events.
  • 岳家 - The husband’s family, specifically the in-laws. This is a traditional term used in classical Chinese literature.
  • 厮仆 - Servants or attendants in the household. These were lower-status individuals who performed domestic tasks.
  • 父兄 - Father and elder brothers, the male heads of the family. This reflects the patriarchal structure of classical Chinese society.
  • 妆资 - The dowry or financial assets brought by a bride into the marriage. This is a key aspect of traditional Chinese marriage customs.
  • 贳马 - To hire or buy a horse. '贳' can mean to borrow or to purchase with one's own funds, depending on context.

Proper Nouns

  • 封 - Feng, a male character.
  • 展 - Zhan, a male character.
  • 大成 - Dacheng, the son of Feng.

  • 释褐为兴化令 - Was appointed magistrate of Xinghua after passing the examination. '释褐' refers to the act of removing one’s commoner’s clothes (the 'brown' or 'humble' clothing) and being appointed to office.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 进身为佣 - Means 'to offer oneself as a servant,' suggesting a humble request for employment.
  • 立券已 - Means 'after the contract was signed,' indicating the formalization of an agreement.
  • 怅然若失 - Means 'sorrowfully as if lost,' expressing a deep sense of loss or regret.
  • 婉应曰 - Means 'responded gently,' indicating a polite or indirect reply.
  • 无恙乎 - Means 'are you well?' or 'are you safe?' a common expression of concern.
  • 不敢谓父死 - Means 'dared not say the father was dead,' indicating a reluctance to speak the truth.
  • 惨然曰 - Means 'said sadly,' expressing a somber or sorrowful tone.
  • 握手悲哀 - Means 'embraced in sorrow,' indicating a deep emotional connection.
  • 导入参其后母 - Means 'led into the presence of his stepmother,' suggesting a formal introduction.
  • 设宴寝门 - Means 'held a banquet at the door to the bedroom,' indicating a private or intimate gathering.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 父无讹 - Refers to the confirmation of paternity, a significant social and familial issue in classical Chinese society.
  • 同乡之故 - Refers to the importance of shared origin or hometown in social and familial relationships.
  • 佣 - Refers to a servant or hired laborer, indicating a lower social status.
  • 后母 - Refers to a stepmother, a common family structure in classical Chinese society.
  • 设宴寝门 - Refers to a private or intimate gathering, often associated with family or close relationships.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '惟日流连其旁，以窥意旨，而其人亦略不顾问' describes prolonged observation and the lack of response from the person being observed.
  • The sequence '如此三日，觇其言笑举止，真父无讹' describes three days of observation and the confirmation of paternity.
  • The clause '即又不敢拜识，乃自陈于群小，求以同乡之故，进身为佣' describes the reluctance to approach directly and the indirect request for employment.
  • The sequence '立券已，主人视其里居、姓氏，似有所动，问所从来' describes the signing of a contract and the owner's reaction to the servant's background.
  • The clause '忠泣诉父名，主人怅然若失，久之，问：“而母无恙乎？”' describes the servant's emotional response and the owner's reaction to the news.
  • The sentence '忠又不敢谓父死，婉应曰：“我父六年前经商不返，母醮而去。幸有伯母抚育，不然，葬沟渎久矣。”' describes the servant's reluctance to speak the truth and the indirect explanation of his situation.
  • The clause '主人惨然曰：“我即是汝父也。”' describes the owner's sorrowful confirmation of his identity.
  • The phrase '于是握手悲哀' describes the emotional reunion.
  • The clause '又导入参其后母' describes the introduction to the stepmother.
  • The sequence '后母姬，年三十余，无出，得忠喜，设宴寝门' describes the stepmother's reaction and the intimate gathering.

  • 每用一合亦效 - '每用' means 'each time used,' '一合' is a unit of measurement, and '亦效' means 'it is also effective.' This phrase suggests that even a small amount of the earth is effective.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 二娘呵之 - Second Madam scolded her; the verb *呵* implies a sharp reprimand or scolding.
  • 始泣曰 - Begged to weep and said; the phrase *始泣曰* indicates that the speaker began to weep and then spoke.
  • 桂儿逼索眼睛 - Gui'er forcibly demanded her eyes; *逼索* is a strong phrase meaning to demand or seize by force.
  • 非解脱，几抉去矣 - Had it not been for release, they would have torn them out; *非解脱* is a conditional clause meaning 'had it not been for liberation'; *抉去* is a strong verb meaning 'to tear out' or 'to pluck out.'
  • 汗粉交下 - Sweat and powder fell together; a metaphor for being deeply ashamed, where *粉* could refer to face powder or blush, indicating a reaction of embarrassment.
  • 漠然 - Indifferently or without emotion; describes a person who is emotionally detached or unresponsive.
  • 合坐寂无一语 - All sat in silence, not uttering a word; *合坐* refers to the group sitting together in silence.
  • 盛妆 - In full attire or in elaborate dress; *盛妆* refers to being dressed in one's finest or most elaborate costume.
  • 独拜李夫人及三姊 - Only paid respects to Lady Li and her third sister; *独拜* indicates that this act of respect was unique or exclusive to these individuals.
  • 买墅者，即程也 - The one who bought the villa was Cheng; *买墅者* is a noun phrase meaning 'the person who bought the villa.'
  • 什物多阙 - Many furnishings were lacking; *什物* refers to household goods or furnishings; *阙* means lacking or incomplete.
  • 赠遗 - Gave as gifts or presented; *赠* is to give, *遗* is to send or give as a gift.
  • 惟李夫人赠一婢受之 - Only Lady Li gave a maid, which she accepted; *惟* indicates exclusivity, *受之* is to accept it.
  • 无何 - Not long after or shortly afterwards; a classical adverb indicating a passage of time.
  • 假归展墓 - Temporarily returned home to visit the tomb; *假归* is to temporarily return home, *展墓* is to visit or pay respects at a tomb.
  • 车马扈从如云 - With a retinue of carriages and horses like clouds; *扈从* means to accompany or escort, *如云* is a simile meaning 'like clouds,' indicating a large number.
  • 礼公柩 - Paid respects to the public coffin; *礼* is to perform ritual, *公柩* is the coffin of a public figure or ancestor.
  • 次参李夫人 - Then paid respects to Lady Li; *次* here indicates a sequence or order.
  • 衣冠既竟 - After dressing in formal attire; *衣冠* refers to ceremonial or formal clothing; *既竟* means 'after completing.'
  • 升舆矣 - Got into the carriage; *升舆* is to enter a carriage.
  • 群公子日竞资财 - The group of sons competed daily for wealth; *日竞* means 'competed daily,' *资财* is wealth or property.
  • 柩之弗顾 - Neglected the coffin; *弗顾* is to not care for or neglect.
  • 灵寝漏败 - The ancestral tomb had leaks and was in disrepair; *灵寝* refers to the tomb or burial chamber, *漏败* means leaking and decaying.
  • 渐将以华屋作山丘矣 - Gradually, it would turn the grand house into a hill; *华屋* is a grand house, *山丘* is a hill or mound.
  • 睹之悲 - Saw it and felt sorrow; *睹* is to see or witness, *悲* is sorrow.
  • 竟不谋于诸郎 - Did not consult the other sons; *竟* is a particle indicating unexpectedness, *谋* is to consult or deliberate.
  • 刻期营葬 - Set a date to arrange the burial; *刻期* is to set a fixed date, *营葬* is to arrange the funeral.
  • 事事尽礼 - Everything was done in accordance with ritual; *事事* is 'every matter,' *尽礼* is to fully observe the rites.
  • 冠盖相属 - Hats and carriages followed one after another; *冠盖* is a metaphor for officials or people of status, *相属* is to follow one after another.
  • 里中咸嘉叹焉 - All in the neighborhood praised and lamented it; *里中* is the neighborhood or local area, *咸* is all, *嘉叹* is to praise and lament.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 程 - The name *Cheng* is likely a surname, possibly a male character in the narrative, the one who bought the villa and later arranged the funeral.
  • 李夫人及三姊 - Lady Li and her third sister, indicating a family relationship. *夫人* is a term of respect for a wife or high-ranking woman.
  • 买墅 - To buy a villa – a common practice in classical Chinese literature, often symbolizing social status or wealth.
  • 什物 - Household goods, often used in classical texts to describe the furnishings of a new home.
  • 赠遗 - Giving gifts, a common social practice in classical Chinese society, especially among the elite.
  • 假归展墓 - A traditional practice of temporarily returning home to pay respects at a family tomb, especially for important events or rituals.
  • 礼公柩 - Paying respects to the coffin of a deceased ancestor, a key ritual in Confucian culture.
  • 群公子 - The group of sons – likely referring to the sons of a family or noble house.
  • 灵寝漏败 - The condition of the tomb, indicating neglect and decay, which was a serious moral and social issue in Confucian society.
  • 华屋作山丘 - A metaphor from classical Chinese literature, suggesting the impermanence of wealth and status, as even grand houses become mounds of earth over time.
  • 营葬 - Arranging a proper burial, which in classical Chinese society was a significant act of filial piety and respect.
  • 事事尽礼 - Adhering to ritual in all matters, a central tenet of Confucian values.
  • 冠盖相属 - A metaphor for a large gathering of officials or people of status, often used to describe a grand funeral or public event.
  • 里中咸嘉叹焉 - The reaction of the local community, which in classical Chinese literature often reflects the moral or social significance of an event.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 痴父不听忠告 - Literally 'foolish father does not listen to loyal advice,' used to describe a father who ignores good counsel.

  • 只有一妪、一婢、一老奴在焉 - Literally 'only an old woman, a maid, and an old servant remained,' indicating the few remaining household members.
  • 周以出死得生，不复追问 - Literally 'Zhou, having escaped death and gained life, no longer pursued the matter,' meaning Zhou chose to forgive and move on.

[967]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 我实供之可矣 - I will confess it then, Zhu's decision to tell the truth.
  • 欲杀夫而娶其妇皆我之为，妇不知之也 - To kill the husband and marry his wife was all my doing; the wife did not know about it, Zhu's confession.
  • 何凭 - What evidence? Asking for proof.
  • 血衣可证 - The blood-stained clothes can prove it, referring to physical evidence.
  • 使人搜诸其家，竟不可得 - Sent people to search his home, but they could not find it, describing the failed search for evidence.
  • 又掠之，死而复苏者再 - Tortured him again, and he died and revived twice, indicating repeated torture.
  • 此母不忍出证据死我耳 - This mother does not want to produce the evidence to kill me, Zhu's speculation about his mother's motives.
  • 待自取之 - Let me get it myself, Zhu's request to retrieve the evidence.
  • 予我衣，死也；即不予，亦死也；均之死，故迟也不如其速也 - Give me the clothes, and I will die; if you do not give them, I will also die; either way, it is death, so why delay? It is better to die quickly, Zhu's reasoning.
  • 令方虑囚 - The magistrate was considering the prisoners, describing the magistrate's role.
  • 忽一人直上公堂 - Suddenly a person walked directly onto the court, describing an unexpected event.
  • 怒目视令而大骂曰 - Glared at the magistrate and shouted loudly, saying, describing the person's reaction.
  • 如此愦愦，何足临民 - So foolish, how can you govern the people? Criticizing the magistrate.
  • 数十辈，将共执之 - Dozens of them, ready to seize him, describing the magistrate's guards.
  • 其人振臂一挥，颓然并仆 - The person waved his arm, and they all fell down, describing the person's supernatural power.
  • 令惧欲逃 - The magistrate was afraid and wanted to escape, describing the magistrate's fear.
  • 我关帝前周将军也 - I am the General Zhou before Lord Guan, identifying the person as a historical figure.
  • 昏官若动，即便诛却 - If the incompetent official moves, I will immediately kill him, threatening the magistrate.
  • 杀人者乃宫标也，于朱某何与 - The killer is Gongbiao; what does Zhu have to do with it? Clarifying the killer's identity.
  • 面无人色 - His face had no color, describing Gongbiao's state after speaking.
  • 及问其人，则宫标也，搒之尽服其罪 - When asked who the person was, it turned out to be Gongbiao, who confessed to his crime under beating, describing the final revelation.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 关帝前周将军 - Refers to General Zhou, a historical figure known for his loyalty and bravery, often associated with Lord Guan (Guan Yu) in Chinese folklore.
  • 宫标 - Refers to Gongbiao, a historical figure or character in the story, who is revealed to be the killer.
  • 朱某 - Refers to Zhu, the person being interrogated in the story.

Proper Nouns

  • 关帝 - Lord Guan (Guan Yu), a historical figure revered as a god in Chinese folk religion.

  • 似恐溪壑之不遽饱，而故假之以岁时也者 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'it seems as if one fears that the ravine and gorge will not be quickly filled, and thus deliberately delays for years,' suggesting a deliberate procrastination.

  • 带一名于纸尾，遂成附骨之疽 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'to add a name to the end of a document, it becomes a cancer that clings to the bone,' indicating the lasting harm of a legal accusation.

  • 自謂過之，不意猶昔耳 - The fox spirit's surprise at its own inability to surpass Ahxiu.

  • 故時復一至，今去矣 - The fox spirit's occasional visits and its current departure.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 狐 - A fox spirit, a common figure in Chinese folklore and mythology, often associated with magical abilities.
  • 阿繡 - A name, possibly a fictional character in the story.
  • 西王母 - The Queen Mother of the West, a significant figure in Chinese mythology, often depicted as a goddess of longevity and immortality.
  • 天宮 - The heavenly palace, a place in Chinese mythology where gods and immortals reside.
  • 前世 - Refers to past lives, a concept in Buddhist and Taoist beliefs.
  • 夭殂 - Refers to premature death, often associated with tragic or unfortunate circumstances.

  • 前见其气色晦黯 - Previously, they saw that the atmosphere was dark and gloomy; '气色' refers to the appearance or atmosphere, and '晦黯' means dark or gloomy.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 瘢痕 - Scars, which can symbolize past trauma or injury.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '长二尺已来' is somewhat ambiguous, as '已来' can mean 'since' or 'already,' but in this context, it likely means 'about' or 'around' two feet tall.
  • The phrase '高亦如之' means 'as tall as the first one.'
  • The phrase '交问何之' is a concise way of saying they asked each other where they were going.
  • The phrase '多罹不吉' is a concise way of saying it was often linked to bad luck.
  • The phrase '二人并无有矣' means they were no longer there after the hunter shouted.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 女坦笑不惊 - The girl calmly laughed without being startled; suggests a composed and serene reaction to an unusual situation.
  • 曳置床上 - Dragged and placed on the bed; *曳* implies a forceful or sudden movement.
  • 拭体干洁 - Wiped her body clean and dry; *拭* is 'to wipe,' *干洁* is 'dry and clean.'
  • 加复被焉 - Covered her with a blanket again; *加* is 'to add' or 'to cover,' *复* is 'again.'
  • 夫人闻之，哭而入 - The lady heard of it, and cried while entering; typical classical structure of *person + verb + while doing something.*
  • 狂婢何杀吾儿 - Mad maid, why did you kill my son? *狂婢* is 'mad maid,' *何* is 'why,' *杀* is 'kill.'
  • 女冁然曰 - The girl smiled joyfully and said; *冁然* is a rare classical term for 'smiling joyfully.'
  • 如此痴儿，不如勿有 - Such a foolish child, it would be better not to have him at all; *痴儿* is 'foolish child,' *不如勿有* is 'better not to have.'
  • 夫人益恚 - The lady became even more angry; *恚* is 'anger' or 'resentment.'
  • 以首触女 - Struck the girl with her head; *触* here is used in the sense of 'to strike' or 'to hit.'
  • 婢辈争曳劝之 - The maids struggled to pull her and persuade her; *婢辈* is 'the maids,' *争曳* is 'struggled to pull,' *劝之* is 'to persuade her.'
  • 方纷噪间 - While the commotion was ongoing; *纷噪* is 'commotion' or 'noisy confusion.'
  • 公子呻矣 - The young master groaned; *呻* is 'to groan' or 'to moan.'
  • 辍涕抚之 - Stopped her tears and comforted him; *辍涕* is 'stopped her tears,' *抚之* is 'to comfort him.'
  • 气息休休 - His breathing was gentle and calm; *休休* is an idiom meaning 'calm' or 'gentle.'
  • 大汗浸淫 - Great sweat seeped through; *浸淫* is 'to seep' or 'to flow gradually.'
  • 沾浃裀褥 - Soaked the mat and quilt; *沾浃* is 'soaked,' *裀褥* is 'mat and quilt.'
  • 食顷汗已 - After a meal's time, the sweat had stopped; *食顷* is 'the time it takes to eat a meal,' used as a measure of time.
  • 似不相识 - Seemed not to recognize them; *似* is 'as if,' *不相识* is 'not recognize.'
  • 言语不痴 - His speech was not foolish; *不痴* is 'not foolish' or 'not dull.'
  • 大异之 - Found it very strange; *异* is 'to find strange' or 'to be astonished.'
  • 携参其父 - Took him to consult his father; *携* is 'to take with,' *参* is 'to consult' or 'to visit,' *其父* is 'his father.'
  • 屡试之果不痴 - Tested him many times, and indeed he was not foolish; *屡试之* is 'tested him many times,' *果不痴* is 'indeed not foolish.'
  • 大喜，如获异宝 - Greatly rejoiced, as if having obtained a rare treasure; *异宝* is 'rare treasure' or 'precious object.'
  • 至晚，还榻故处 - By evening, he returned to his old bed; *至晚* is 'by evening,' *还榻* is 'returned to the bed,' *故处* is 'old place.'
  • 更设衾枕以觇之 - Set up a new bed and pillow to observe him; *更设* is 'set up anew,' *衾枕* is 'blanket and pillow,' *觇之* is 'to observe him.'
  • 尽遣婢去 - Sent all the maids away; *尽遣* is 'sent all away,' *婢去* is 'maids to leave.'
  • 早窥之，则榻虚设 - In the morning, they looked, and the bed was empty; *早窥之* is 'looked in the morning,' *榻虚设* is 'the bed was set empty.'
  • 自此痴颠皆不复作 - From then on, his madness and delirium never occurred again; *痴颠* is 'foolishness and madness,' *不复作* is 'no longer occurred.'
  • 而琴瑟静好如形影焉 - And the relationship was peaceful and harmonious, like shadow and form; *琴瑟* is a metaphor for marital harmony, *静好* is 'peaceful and good,' *如形影* is 'like shadow and form.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions
